item.title: Just calling a spade a spade
item.description: Sorry, not sorry
item.dc:creator: Johje19

item.title: I got my guts rearranged, and not even in a fun way.
item.description:
I don’t know if this breaks the selfie rule, but 1. WTF even is this app anymore? 2. What I want to say is important to anyone with a uterus and related organs. Or anyone who loves someone with a uterus and related organs. So please at least read this before you downvote. 3. I feel like all I see is hot women in thigh highs and sexy cosplay. I enjoy both, but don’t understand how this would be unacceptable in comparison. I want to share a summary of my Endometriosis journey here. If even one person suffering from this disease (or diseases like PCOS, Adenomyosis, etc) sees this post and either finds strength to advocate for themselves, or has suffered these symptoms without realizing these diseases even exist, then it’s worth whatever “rules” I’m breaking. Hi. I’m Bri. I’m 34. I’ve battled with Endo for 22 years. Literally since I began menstruating at 12 years old. It took 18 years for me to get a diagnosis. I only got that diagnosis because I had been fighting with my obgyn for 5 years to give me a tubal ligation. She denied me every year, because “what if my future husband wanted children”? Yes, a nonexistent man had more say and claim over my body than I did. And I live in Mass, one of the most liberal state in the US. She left the practice and my new doctor granted me my tubal immediately. I had spent days writing a speech demanding the tubal and rehearsed it for days as well. My appointment was on the day Texas first deliberated and passed their oppressive anti-abortion laws. I planned to refuse to leave the office until she agreed. She and my nurse mid-wife both agreed to give me the surgery without any of my speech or hesitation on their part. I was almost disappointed at not being able to give my impassioned speech on which I’d worked so hard. But more grateful that they enthusiastically agreed. And when I told them about my former doctor denying me and why, they were visibly concerned and said they would have a discussion with the rest of the practice. But I digress….
I got my tubal at 30. The only way to diagnose Endometriosis is laparoscopically. Since this was a laparoscopic surgery, they found lesions on my bladder, tubes, and bowels. Because I had only consented to the tubal, there was nothing they could do at that time. My doctor told me she had found Endo when I woke up. I was in an anesthetic haze and it didn’t really register until my 2 week post-op appointment when she informed me of how much she had seen and asked how painful my periods were (as if I hadn’t been telling my former obgyn for years- turns out she left nothing in my medical notes- including my requests every year for the tubal). While I was grateful for a diagnosis that finally explained a lot of the symptoms I’d been battling for years, I was also frustrated, because this doctor pretty much brushed me off after telling me. Like, oh you have Endo, I had tacos for lunch, good luck! And I was angry at every doctor who told me my pain and perfuse bleeding was normal when my symptoms were literally textbook for Endo. I went off my birth control (pretty much the only non-surgical treatment for Endo, which I didn’t know I had when I started taking it), and over the next year, my symptoms quickly became insufferable to the point that I was bedridden some days. I asked my doctor for care, and she told me it wasn’t a specialty of hers, so she couldn’t help, and I would need to call my insurance company to find a specialist in my network. Again, Mass is great for subsidized healthcare… to a degree. Because I could not find a specialist in network for a long time. When I finally did, they were booked out for 8 months just for a consultation.
For 22 years, I battled unmanageable bleeding and pain so blindingly bad I would vomit and pass out. For 22 years, I had to live through severe gastrointestinal and urological problems that were just shrugged off by specialists. For 18 of those year, I was told by every single doctor I had, that all of this was normal and that being a woman just sucks. There were days I couldn’t even drink water without vomiting from pain and nausea. And I was told that was normal and I probably had a low pain threshold. I have broken bones, and had injuries that required stitches- and none of those even remotely compared to my period pain. For 4 more years, my symptoms got worse and worse each month. It got to the point where I was filling a 30ml menstrual cup in 3 hours (the average blood loss for people menstruating is 30-60ml… over their entire period! I was losing that much every 3-6 hours for 2 days of my period). My pain would radiate from my lower abdomen and back, all the way down to my kneecaps and up to my shoulders. There were days I contemplated going to the ER because I literally couldn’t stand from the pain- but I couldn’t afford the copay and figured they’d do nothing for me anyway. It got to the point where I was bleeding an average of 15-20 days per month. Endo had taken so much of my life. Countless opportunities missed because I was in too much pain, or was bleeding unmanageably. It’s hard not to wonder how much further along I could be in my life if this disease hadn’t taken so much of it. It’s gut wrenching (Ha! Pun intended) to think of all the times I just had to “push through” the pain and profuse bleeding and just go to school and work because I had no fucking choice and no substantial support system. Meanwhile, lesions were pushing through most of my abdominal organs and into my chest. *Warning! Don’t scroll down to the next photo if you don’t want to see a picture of the lesions on my diaphragm. They’re not graphic, but it is a photo of my guts.* Finally, after years of suffering and seeing different doctors and specialists, and having multiple MRIs and Ultrasounds, including a transvaginal Ultrasound - I found the right surgeon who believed my pain and believed I should have surgery, despite all of my scans being completely clean. This, to me, is one of the most insidious parts of Endometriosis. With the exception of significant nodular growths, most lesions and adhesions don’t show up on any sort of imaging. Like I said, laparoscopy is the only way to truly see and diagnose it.
A month ago tomorrow, I finally underwent the surgery I’d fought for for years. I had a total hysterectomy and most of the adhesions in my abdomen were removed. This will not cure me. Endo is a full body, chronic inflammatory disease with chronic pain. There is a good chance that adhesions and lesions will return until I go through menopause. I was in stage IV- the worst stage on the current metric for gauging Endometriosis. I still need to have 2 more surgeries in the near future. One is because I have close to 20 lesions on my diaphragm, as you can see above. Because it is the barrier to the thoracic cavity, it requires a cardio thoracic surgeon and my obgyn surgeon could not remove those. My liver is also plastered to my diaphragm and needs to be removed from it. This second surgery will require a chest tube and I will be in the hospital for a few days. I also need a coil placed on the blood supply to my ovaries. Basically, the blood supply to both ovaries is massively engorged, while the supply out of the ovary is normal. So I still have severe ovarian pain after surgery, because the blood supply is so massive. I’m also at higher risk for a rupture which would cause internal bleeding. If the coil does not work, I could lose one or both ovaries. I also still have sharp shoulder pain, due to the lesions still on my diaphragm.
The point of posting this whole long story is as I mentioned in the beginning. If you read all of this, I thank you. But I just want people to know that severe menstrual pain is not normal. Sever gastrointestinal and urological problems are not normal. Severe bleeding is not normal. And if you keep getting medically gaslit by your dr, try if you can to find a new one. My current surgeon is a god damn saint. She literally saved my life, or at least is helping me save it by relieving me of some pain and mostly of the bleeding. She was the first one to really listen to me! And believe my pain! It’s hard as fuck to stand up for yourself, especially as a woman, especially against doctors. But nobody else is going to advocate for you. It’s an exhausting fight and I often felt like Sisyphus. But for me, the alternative to that fight was suffering until I couldn’t bear it and continuing to lose my life. Thank you for reading. If you want to talk about any of this, or have any questions, please reach out. Much love, from my messy guts and me.
Atlas at the beach tax.
item.dc:creator: AtlasHuggedrescuepup

item.dc:creator: Snooj

item.title: He deserved exactly what he got.
item.description: The pearl clutching has to end. There should be absolutely no equivocation about condemning this man and celebrating his death. He was a violent white supremacist who spent his entire life advocating for white supremacy, advocating the murder of all queer people, and advocating fascism. He was a nazi shitstain and if you're not glad he's dead, if you "condemn all violence", if you begin any criticism of him with "of course he didn't deserve this" then you are a fascist sympathizer. He did deserve this. Our country is run by fascists like this. Their ongoing existence is a threat to innocent people, a threat to democracy, and a threat to the freedom of all Americans. I am so tired of people using this opportunity to "condemn" violence as though all violence were the same. Violence against fascists is self defense. And if this, as some have suggested, somehow turns out to be some kind of 'inside job' by a conservative assassin, then I'm a little sad it wasn't done for the right reasons, but still happy that there's one less fascist, and you should be too, because if their plan was for this to turn people against the left then that plan is already backfiring, because most Americans absolutely despise people like Kirk. And one last thing: fascists are already violent, they alfeady control our government, courts, and media, they are already trying to enslave and murder innocent people, they have already undermined the government. If you think that they were holding anything back, if you think that this will be the thing that finally "gives them an excuse", if you think that they are only now dangerous, then you clearly come from a place of immense privilege, because targeted minorities like me have understood for a long time that these people have never needed an excuse and they will never stop, not ever, until people stand up and stop them. One fascist was stopped last night, permanently. That can only be a good thing.
item.dc:creator: Freyja33

item.title: Good morning!
item.description:
Slider theme is a great addition
In personal news, I had a home nurse visit for all of 2 weeks before I got discharged. She called my recovery "miraculous". So yeah, I'm doing pretty good. Also saw a Tesla hit another car in a parking lot lol.

item.title: There are Liberals who think we should mourn the loss of Julius Streicher. I disagree.
item.description: Except for the last one, these were all stolen from a post on bluesky, but I don't feel comfortable providing the source-tweet in case there's any backlash or harassment by fascists or civility-warrior centrists who ultimately serve fascists with their greater hostility towards Leftists and progressives than they have towards said fascists. My condolences to political snake, Gavin Newsom, for the loss of his dear friend and kindred spirit.

item.title: Ever seen a Binturong before
item.description:
The binturong, or "bearcat," is a Southeast Asian mammal from the civet family, known for its long, bushy, prehensile tail and a distinctive smell like buttered popcorn. These nocturnal, arboreal animals are omnivores, primarily eating fruit but also consuming insects, small animals, and eggs. Binturongs are solitary, use their tails for climbing and balance, and play a vital role in their ecosystem by spreading the seeds of strangler fig trees.
Face of the Binturong or bearcat
item.dc:creator: wolfwinter

item.title: You're definitely going to want to unmute
item.dc:creator: jayman0123

item.title: I miss both of you.
item.description: 24 years ago today, at the age of three, I lost both of my parents. Based on the date, it wouldn't take much guessing to figure out what happened. This year, I'm going to assume everyone is familiar with the event and not get too much into it. Today, I'm in Nee York City visiting the memorial, I uploaded this generic photo of it because I'm turning my phone off, I'm not taking a camera, and I'm just cutting the outside world off for a few hours to be alone with my siblings. I wanted to write the following to my parents, I realize it might be silly to you all, but it helps me. Mom, dad; This last January, your triplet grandsons were born. I can already tell their older sisters are going to help turn them into proper gentlemen. I realize she's not really family by blood, but I'd like to tell you that my stepdaughter started med school in Tokyo this year. I met her when she was 10, but it feels like she's mine anyway. My oldest and the twins start school in Japan as soon as we leave NYC. I reunited with mom's sister and mother, and they want all of my kids to spend some time over there. They've all become obsessed with cooking, I have a feeling they're going to open up a family restaurant one day. My nieces will be joining them in Japanese school. I have a feeling they're going to grow up and be involved in the space industry like their mom. They're already obsessed with watching rockets on TV. My older siblings all got back from deployment. They're all fighter pilots like you, dad, and I think you would approve of my sister's new husband, even though he's a jarhead. Not much is new with the rest of us this year, other than moving closer to home. But on the bright side, there's not much to worry about. We're all doing good. I'm going to get dressed and go now, I'm going to visit the memorial. I love you both.
item.dc:creator: DrElizabethTakeda

item.title: Even wild cheetahs need love.
item.description: This video shows a 100% wild cheetah. They're known for wandering up to humans and just chilling. There are no recorded deaths from cheetahs and very, very few attacks.

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1471 (sauropod shuffle)
item.description:
Let's get this bread or something
Pippin had a good time when I took him to the Museum of Northern Arizona, but he's not allowed back to hang with the ancestors, which i think is mildly racist.
Though one of his favorite chew toys is literally a dinosaur bone piece he picked out at a rock shop in Utah, so I guess I get why?
Trying to apply for food stamp benefits today so as to stretch my tiny finances further, but the website is being a whiny little bitch and not sending me a PIN. "PIN has been sent via phone or email" No it hasn't, bitch, it's lost in the goddamn nebula and I haven't received it because your website is made and run by republican douches who would rather I die then recieve a crumb of aid from the State because yall are so Sumerian/Fascist about what your definition of the State should be responsible for.
Guess I'll have to bite the bullet and wait three hours to talk to an actual person after I've posted this to figure out what's wrong. Only to be told that they can't because of some bullshit esoteric law from 1912.
Honestly, Grindr should just do this anyway.
Asshole in the corner can't even spell Thucydides right. No wonder he's become an incel. Well, that, and he's insufferable.
Just Sauropod Things
I'm down.
Probably should have saved this for a Bronze-age dump but idc
I like my food to make me sweat.
If I had a kid or two that used silverware I'd totally get these.
Field Artillery might also be a good way, but that really didn't occur to the Greeks.
Accurate. Though just as often if I leave the cage door open and put Pippin in the cage for the night, he crawls into bed and down into my shirt over my heart to sleep anyway. When I nap, he usually stands guard over my headboard.
Shrimp. Not even once.
What an odd Ankylosaur.
I hope people in my game like the random dinosaurs.
MY CABBAGES says Diocletian.
The Boii were a Celtic people from roughly where the modern Czech Republic is.
Nuthatches are cute birbs.
I love that it's merely "repel bad luck." Yeah, that's all the dismembered parts of the Savior are good for. Incidentally, I now have the idea for a horror DnD campaign....
Either that or its history or birbs.
I really want to see a Yellow-headed Blackbird.
Ah yes, good old Amphicoelias fragillimus, once posited to maybe perhaps be the longest Sauropod ever, if related to Diplodocus, somewhere between 130 and 200 feet long... the vertebrae has vanished, and all we have are Cope's drawings. Which were good enough to allow a re-assessment, and it's been now renamed Maraapunisarus fragillimus and reassessed as a rebbachisaurid sauropod. It was still bloody fucking large, though, probably was now a mere 115 feet long. I wish more fossils of this critter would be found.
I had a dream during my nap yesterday that a Ruff (a bizarre shorebird) was found near Winslow. My rare bird dreams, for some reason, have an odd tendency to come to pass, so im watching ebird alerts like a hawk.
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: "I'm sorry. What? Merp. Pippin." ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 3) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 4) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride 5) Since multiple people have requested this over the last two weeks or so, my ko-fi is: https://ko-fi.com/codyandpippin
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: History repeats over again and again.
item.description:
All these quotes
All the protests going on
Yet here we stand
Making memes
What changes are we making?
This world is only getting darker

item.title: Not all the lambs MAGA sacrifices (like Charlie Kirk) will stop us from remembering the EPSTEIN FILES. Disclose the files!
item.description:
Utah is a very much MAGA State. The absolute majority of the students at that college were white nationalist and fascist Charlie Kirk fans. The shooter suddenly vanished, after some “random” arrest.
WHY ARE YOU MAGAS SO OBVIOUS?! Heritage Foundation in enacting steps of Project 2025 mimicking Beyonce Okay, okay, ladies Now let's get in formation ('cause I slay) Okay, ladies Now let's get in formation ('cause I slay) Prove to me you got some coordination ('cause I slay) Slay trick, or you get eliminated (I slay)
The coordination is embarrassingly obvious. They are itching to impose martial law, forfeit the upcoming election, and get away with time travel from 1865 backwards.
Mocking for thee, not for me! Lunatic ultra conservatives in a nutshell.
IT IS A FALSE FLAG! Charlie Kirk was as much a sacrificial lamb as Jeffrey Epstein did not kill himself.
How did he know he had to give condolences for the death of someone who as was still alive at the time of the recording?
item.dc:creator: shapeshifter69

item.title: She's gonna be up at 3am thinking about that moment
item.dc:creator: AnceDance
